signals.headline: Trump Announces Two-Week Ceasefire with Iran Ahead of 8 p
signals.raw:
Trump Announces Two-Week Ceasefire with Iran Ahead of 8 p.m. Deadline | YES | HIGH confidence** [1][4]

President Donald Trump announced on Tuesday evening (April 7, 2026) that the U.S. reached a two-week ceasefire agreement with Iran, just before his self-imposed 8 p.m. ET deadline, averting threatened strikes on Iranian civilian infrastructure like bridges, power plants, and desalination plants.

signals.headline: The BOK expects inflation to rise to the **mid to upper two-percent range** for the year, driven largely by surging oil
signals.raw: The BOK expects inflation to rise to the **mid to upper two-percent range** for the year, driven largely by surging oil costs following the Iran conflict[4].

signals.headline: HEADLINE: JD Vance faces backlash after openly campaigning for losing Putin-aligned Hungarian leader Orbán, spotlighting
signals.raw:
HEADLINE: JD Vance faces backlash after openly campaigning for losing Putin-aligned Hungarian leader Orbán, spotlighting GOP silence ahead of midterms** | **YES** | **MED**  
Vance’s unprecedented Hungary trip last week aided Orbán’s far-right Fidesz party, which trailed polls and lost substantially, underscoring U.S. turnout lessons for Democrats against Trump/MAGA in midterms and 2028[1][1].

signals.headline: BOK HOLDS RATE AT 2
signals.raw:
BOK HOLDS RATE AT 2.5% FOR 7TH CONSECUTIVE MONTH; MARCH INFLATION AT 2.2%** | YES | HIGH confidence

On April 10, 2026, the **Bank of Korea's Monetary Policy Board unanimously decided to maintain its benchmark interest rate at 2.5%** for the seventh consecutive month[4]. This decision was widely anticipated given economic conditions.

signals.headline: **March 2026 inflation reached 2
signals.raw: **March 2026 inflation reached 2.2%**, up from 2.0% in the previous two months but below market forecasts of 2.4%[1]. This marks a 5-month high for the year[1].

signals.headline: The Policy Board noted that **anticipated economic growth would fall below 2%** due to won weakness (exchange rate near
signals.raw: The Policy Board noted that **anticipated economic growth would fall below 2%** due to won weakness (exchange rate near 1,520 won per dollar)[4].

signals.headline: Core inflation remained stable at **2
signals.raw: Core inflation remained stable at **2.0%**, in line with the central bank's 2% target[1].

signals.headline: Market-moving implications:** The combination of higher-than-expected March inflation (2
signals.raw: Market-moving implications:** The combination of higher-than-expected March inflation (2.2% vs. 2.0% forecast) alongside persistent external risks (oil prices, currency weakness) confirms the BOK's hawkish hold, suggesting the easing cycle has ended and rates will remain at 2.5% for an extended period[3].

signals.headline: HEADLINE: Marjorie Taylor Greene predicts GOP “bloodbath” in 2026 midterms, blasts Trump and Republican Party in fresh i
signals.raw:
HEADLINE: Marjorie Taylor Greene predicts GOP “bloodbath” in 2026 midterms, blasts Trump and Republican Party in fresh interview** | **YES** | **MED**  
Greene’s new Politico interview signals internal GOP fractures that could shift midterm odds amid Democratic turnout momentum[2][2].
